href=\"https://catalog.ucmerced.edu/preview_entity.php?catoid=24&ent_oid=1040\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">catalog</a> page for more information about the Computer Science and Engineering<strong> </strong>major.</p><p><br></p><p>In addition to the Major Selection Criteria, all Upper-Division Transfer applicants must meet minimum <a href=\"https://admission.universityofcalifornia.edu/admission-requirements/transfer-requirements/preparing-to-transfer/basic-requirements.html\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">University of California admissions requirements</a>. Visit <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/requirements</a> for more specific UC Merced admissions information.</p><p><br></p><p>Prior to Transferring to UC Merced, please be advised of the following for Junior Transfers:</p><p><br></p><p><strong>1) WRI 001 and WRI 010 are admissions requirements:</strong> WRI 001 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1A - English Composition. WRI 010 is fulfilled by IGETC/Cal-GETC 1B - Critical Thinking/English Composition. Please scroll towards the bottom of the agreement to find the articulation for each course.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>2) Transfer Admissions Guarantee (TAG):</strong> UC Merced is one of the six UC's that offers Transfer Admissions Guarantee. Please visit the TAG website for more information: <a href=\"https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">https://admissions.ucmerced.edu/transfer/tag</a></p><p><br></p><p><strong>3) General Education (GE Requirements):</strong> While general education is not required for admission, it can help speed up your time to graduation once you transfer to UC Merced. We highly recommend reviewing the <a href=\"https://catalog.ucmerced.edu/content.php?catoid=22&navoid=2163#transfer\" rel=\"noopener noreferrer\" target=\"_blank\">Transfer Students: Satisfying General Education</a> page in the catalog for a more extensive explanation of the requirements.</p><p><br></p><p>Please note the School of Engineering strongly discourages IGETC, but accepts it; students are encouraged to focus primarily on lower division major preparation and fulfilling UCM minimum admissions requirements. If you elect to complete IGETC, the courses may double count with the major courses listed below. Please visit your Community College Counselor to learn more.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>4. This agreement displays all lower-division (or Major Preparation) courses required in the major.</strong> UC Merced courses on the left, approved (articulated) transfer courses to the right.</p><p><br></p><p><strong>5. Changes to this Agreements:</strong> Major requirements are subject to change from one academic year to the next. Newly-articulated courses are added on a rolling basis, and articulated courses can be revised.
